reasoning: 'Sentimental Value' is the upcoming film by acclaimed Norwegian director Joachim Trier, expected to release in 2025 and compete at the 98th Academy Awards. While Trier is highly respected and his previous film 'The Worst Person in the World' received Oscar nominations, international and non-English-language films have historically had very low win rates for the top prize of Best Picture ('Parasite' being a rare exception). Even with a potential strong distributor like NEON, the base rate for any single contender in November winning Best Picture is roughly 2-5%, and for an international arthouse drama, it is at the lower end or below that range. Lacking overwhelming evidence of it being the definitive frontrunner of the year, a probability of 2% reflects its slim chances of winning the ultimate award amidst heavy competition from major American studio and prestige films.
